name: Ingush
notes: Ingush (гӀалгӀай мотт), a Nakh (Northeast Caucasian) language of Ingushetia, Russia. STUB: the grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. Script: Cyrillic alphabet (since 1938), which writes the large Nakh consonant inventory with multigraphs — ejectives with the palochka ⟨Ӏ⟩ (кӀ, пӀ, тӀ, цӀ, чӀ), pharyngeals ⟨гӀ, хӀ, аӀ⟩, uvulars ⟨къ, хь, хъ⟩ — so a correct spec needs a multigraph-aware table plus the vowel-length and nasalisation rules; several digraphs are ambiguous with letter sequences. Input is assumed to be the Cyrillic orthography, with the palochka written as U+04C0 ⟨Ӏ⟩ (not a Latin capital I or a digit 1). PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode ingu1240), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.
